action.reasoning: Observation: dataset discovery now works and training starts, but the collator crashes because this feature extractor does not return attention_mask by default. Analysis: wav2vec2 feature extractors often need return_attention_mask=True at load time/call time, and we should robustly handle missing masks by creating all-ones masks. Also train.py appears truncated in workspace, so safest is to rewrite complete train.py and align predict.py with the same mask fallback. Decision: patch train/predict collators to request and synthesize attention masks, keep the short 2-epoch baseline, test predict locally on dev subset, then submit eval immediately if successful.
action.command:
python train.py --epochs 2 --batch_size 32 --num_workers 6 --save_path checkpoints/baseline.pt && python - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
import shutil
root=Path('/rab_data/asvspoof2021_la/dev')
out=Path('tmp_pred_data')
(out/'flac').mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
ids=[]
for p in sorted((root/'flac').glob('*.flac'))[:64]:
    ids.append(p.stem)
    shutil.copy2(p, out/'flac'/p.name)
with open(out/'test.txt','w') as f:
    f.write('\n'.join(ids)+'\n')
print('prepared', len(ids))
PY
python predict.py --data_path tmp_pred_data --checkpoint checkpoints/baseline.pt --output tmp_pred.json --batch_size 32 --num_workers 2 && python - <<'PY'
import json
x=json.load(open('tmp_pred.json'))
print('num_preds', len(x['predictions']), 'first', x['predictions'][0])
PY
